name: Sorting
description: Questions on sorting algorithms and Python's sort.

questions.question: Python's built-in sort uses which algorithm?
questions.explanation: Python uses Timsort, a hybrid derived from merge sort and insertion sort, with O(n log n) worst case.

questions.question: What is the key difference between list.sort() and sorted(list)?
choices: sort() mutates in place; sorted() returns a new list
questions.explanation: list.sort() sorts in place and returns None; sorted(iterable) returns a new sorted list and leaves the original unchanged.

questions.question: To sort a list of tuples by the second element, you would use:
questions.explanation: key=lambda x: x[1] tells sort to compare using the second element of each tuple.

questions.question: What is the best typical time complexity of comparison-based sorting?
questions.explanation: Comparison-based sorts have a lower bound of Omega(n log n); optimal algorithms achieve O(n log n).

questions.question: sorted([(1,'b'),(2,'a')], key=lambda x: x[1]) returns:
questions.explanation: Sorting by the second element (string) gives alphabetical order: 'a' before 'b', so (2,'a') comes first.
